items.mistake: Mixing pyplot state and OO style in the same function
items.fix: Pick one style per function: create fig, ax = plt.subplots() and use only ax methods, or only plt.* after plt.figure().
items.why: plt.gca() may not be the axes you think after subplots—labels and data land on the wrong panel.
items.example: After fig, axes = plt.subplots(2, 1), call axes[0].plot(...) not plt.plot(...) unless you explicitly plt.sca(axes[0]).

items.mistake: Calling plt.show() in headless scripts without saving first
items.fix: Call fig.savefig(...) before show(); in batch jobs skip show() and rely on Agg backend.
items.why: show() blocks and displays nothing useful on servers; unsaved figures are lost when the process exits.
items.example: In a training script: fig.savefig('loss.png', dpi=150); do not depend on show() on a remote GPU node.

items.mistake: Plotting without axis labels or units on ML EDA figures
items.fix: Always ax.set_xlabel / set_ylabel with feature names and units; ax.set_title with dataset or split context.
items.why: Unlabeled axes make notebook outputs unusable in reports and confuse collaborators reviewing EDA.
items.example: Use ax.set_xlabel('Age (years)') not ax.set_xlabel('x') on a histogram of a demographic feature.

items.mistake: Using plt.figure() repeatedly without closing figures in a loop
items.fix: Reuse one fig/ax, or call plt.close(fig) after savefig in loops generating many plots.
items.why: Each open figure consumes memory; long hyperparameter sweeps can exhaust RAM.
items.example: for k in ks: fig, ax = plt.subplots(); ...; fig.savefig(f'k{k}.png'); plt.close(fig)
